curation_notes: Re-curation. The advisory references two commits: d670750 (PR #493, prevents arbitrary EL expression evaluation in CronValidator) and cfd2880 (PR #494, removes user expression from CronParser error messages). Used d670750 as the patch commit since it fixes the primary vulnerability. Pre-patch commit 6f91560 is its first parent. Added CronParser.java:parse as a second location because in the pre-patch code it embeds the user-controlled cron expression into the error message (via String.format with '%s'), which is then propagated through the exception chain to the EL template evaluation in CronValidator.isValid(). Vulnerability class kept as command-injection: while technically an EL injection (CWE-94), the EL expressions enable arbitrary OS command execution via Runtime.exec(), and command-injection is the closest of the 13 valid classes.
